{{Quote|Citadel Station is at a crossroads right now, you know. And so are you. Don't take the wrong road.|Jana Lorso to Meetra Surik|<ref name="KOTOR2" />}}
'''Jana Lorso''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Mirialan/Legends|Mirialan]] [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]] executive who worked for the [[Telos IV]] [[Citadel Station]] Czerka branch during the [[Jedi Civil War]]. In [[3951 BBY]], Lorso oversaw Czerka's involvement in the [[Telosian Restoration Project]] after [[Saul Karath]] bombarded the planet, and had help from the [[Bumani Exchange Corporation]] and [[Loppak Slusk]] during Czerka's early involvement in the Project. She was highly disliked by her own employees and the [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] that she hired for protection of her office, which eventually led to one of them giving up credentials to the [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorians]] on the station so they could steal [[B-4D4-GE3]].

Lorso's interference with the Telos project was not looked upon kindly by the Ithorians who ran the project. Initially, Lorso contacted [[Meetra Surik]] shortly after she arrived on Citadel Station, requesting a meeting to discuss a job opportunity. After meeting with Lorso, Surik rejected Lorso's offer, and instead decided to work for the Ithorians. The Ithorian leader, [[Chodo Habat]], hired Surik to [[Skirmishes on Citadel Station|deal]] with Czerka three times. The first time, Surik dealt with Czerka mercenaries inside the docking bay, securing an information droid to replace [[G0-T0|one that had mysteriously vanished]]. The second time, she stole B-4D4 and essential Czerka documents from the mainframe, right under Lorso's nose. Finally, Surik and her companions helped the Ithorians when Lorso sent her mercenaries to [[Skirmish in the Ithorian Compound|invade]] the Ithorian compound.

Lorso would also be responsible for the luring in of three bounties. One was a man named [[Batono]], a former Czerka employee who wanted to expose her and Czerka's crimes to the [[Telosian Council]], though Lorso threatened to silence him if he spoke. When she told Surik that he spent time with the Ithorians, Surik brought Batono in to the station authorities. Batono helped expose Lorso and Czerka's crimes. Before Batono was brought in, she hired two wanted fugitives—a [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] and a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]]—to the surface of Telos where Surik would [[Skirmish on Telos|confront]] and kill them later when she was forced to the surface in search of the [[Ebon Hawk]]. After Surik left the station finally, Lorso and Czerka were forced to leave as well.

On a lighter note, her employees on Citadel Station imply that she was sending some of her supplies to [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], to fund [[Azkul]]'s [[Azkul's mercenaries|mercenaries]] with their [[Exchange]] allies in order to take down the [[Khoonda|local government]]. This changed in [[3951 BBY]], when Azkul's mercenaries were defeated at the [[Battle of Khoonda]] and the local Exchange thugs and Czerka employees were driven off the planet in the aftermath of the battle.

==Behind the scenes==
She was voiced by [[Elizabeth Conboy]].

The Exile can side with Czerka and Jana Lorso if the player wishes to play on a darker path. This alternate story includes missions for Jana Lorso, such as stealing the Ithorians' [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] and rescuing Lorso from her own mercenaries.

Later in the game's "dark side" path, Lorso was supposed to take control of the station thanks to Surik's actions on the station and [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]]. Time constraints forced Obsidian to remove this event from the game.
